claim: For all integers n >= 1, the number of divisors of n is odd if and only if n is a perfect square.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square then sigma(n) is odd.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square then phi(n) is even.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square then sigma(n) and phi(n) are coprime.
For all integers n >= 1, tau(n) is odd if and only if n is a perfect fourth power.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect fourth power then sigma(n) is divisible by 5.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect fourth power then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect fourth power then sigma(n) and phi(n) are coprime.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree then tau(n) is 2^k where k is the number of distinct prime factors of n.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1) if is_prime(p))) <= (tau(n) == 2**(sum(1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1) if is_prime(p) and vp(n,p)==1)))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree then sigma(n) is odd.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1) if is_prime(p))) <= (sigma(n) % 2 == 1))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree then sigma(n) is divisible by n.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1)) <= (sigma(n) % n == 0))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square and squarefree then n=1.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((int(abs(n)**0.5)**2 == n and vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1))) <= (n == 1))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree then phi(n) is even unless n=2.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1)) <= ((n==2) or (phi(n)%2==0)))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree and phi(n) is even then n is composite.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1)) and phi(n)%2==0) <= (n > 1 and not is_prime(n)))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square and squarefree then n=1 and sigma(n)=1.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((int(abs(n)**0.5)**2 == n and vp(n,2)==0 and all(vp(n,p)==0 or vp(n,p)==1 for p in range(2,int(abs(n)**0.5)+1))) <= (n == 1 and sigma(n) == 1))
